title: Swartzia peruviana
taxonomy: Swartzia peruviana is classified within the family Leguminosae, commonly referred to as the legume or pea family. This family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes a wide variety of species, including trees, shrubs, and herbaceous plants. The Leguminosae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Swartzia peruviana belonging to the subfamily Faboideae. The Faboideae subfamily is characterized by its unique floral structure and is often referred to as the true peas or broad beans. As part of the Faboideae subfamily, Swartzia peruviana shares characteristics with other plants in this group.
uses: Swartzia peruviana is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties.  Swartzia peruviana has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, including treating fever and other infections. intro: Swartzia peruviana, also known as Peruvian Swartzia, is a plant species belonging to the family Leguminosae, commonly found in the tropical regions of the Americas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate various health conditions such as fever, pain, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Swartzia peruviana include flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used as a raw material in the production of herbal supplements and pharmaceuticals.
